---
title: "Home"
image: assets/images/og-image.jpg
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.hero-text}
<div class="kicker">Research profile</div>

# Dr.-Ing. Tobias Recker

<div class="hero-meta">Postdoctoral Researcher · Institute of Assembly Technology and Robotics · Leibniz University Hannover</div>

<p class="lead">I develop scalable cooperative mobile multi-robot systems for industrial handling, assembly, and construction automation.</p>

I design control, path-planning, and system-integration approaches that allow multiple mobile robots and mobile manipulators to cooperate as one adaptable robotic system — especially for large-scale components that are difficult to handle with a single robot.

<div class="button-row">
<a class="button primary" href="research.html">Explore research</a>
<a class="button" href="projects.html">View projects</a>
<a class="button" href="assets/pdfs/recker-dissertation-2025.pdf">Download dissertation</a>
</div>
:::

::: {.hero-portrait-wrap}
<img class="hero-portrait" src="assets/images/tobias-recker-portrait.jpg" alt="Portrait of Tobias Recker">
:::
:::

## Research focus

<p class="section-intro">My work is centered on robotic systems that become more useful when they cooperate: robots that share objects, coordinate motion, and scale with task size instead of being designed as single-purpose machines.</p>

::: {.card-grid}
::: {.card}
### Cooperative mobile multi-robot systems
Scalable control frameworks for mobile robots and mobile manipulators that jointly transport, handle, and assemble large-scale components.
:::

::: {.card}
### Mobile manipulation and control
Coordination of mobile platforms and robotic arms, including formation control, trajectory planning, and whole-body control.
:::

::: {.card}
### Automation for construction and production
Robotic automation for flexible manufacturing, large-scale assembly, and mobile additive manufacturing.
:::
:::

## Featured projects

::: {.card-grid}
::: {.project-card}
<a class="video-thumb" href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7jzTUw5pK40" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
<img src="assets/images/thumb-object-handling.jpg" alt="Cooperative object handling with eight mobile manipulators">
<span class="play-button">▶</span>
</a>
<div class="project-card-body">
<div class="meta">Cooperative handling</div>
### Cooperative object handling
Multiple mobile manipulators jointly grasp and move one object in a spatial workspace.
</div>
:::

::: {.project-card}
<a class="video-thumb" href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f0-cd06wfmM" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
<img src="assets/images/thumb-object-transport.jpg" alt="Cooperative object transport with mobile robot platforms">
<span class="play-button">▶</span>
</a>
<div class="project-card-body">
<div class="meta">Object transport</div>
### Cooperative object transport
Mobile platforms coordinate formation motion to transport large industrial components.
</div>
:::

::: {.project-card}
<a class="video-thumb" href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IAT_a7R-n6Y" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
<img src="assets/images/thumb-additive-manufacturing.jpg" alt="Mobile additive manufacturing with mobile manipulators">
<span class="play-button">▶</span>
</a>
<div class="project-card-body">
<div class="meta">Construction automation</div>
### Print-while-drive additive manufacturing
A mobile manipulator coordinates platform and arm motion for continuous large-scale additive manufacturing.
</div>
:::
:::

<div class="link-list">
<a href="projects.html">All project details →</a>
</div>

## Selected publication

::: {.paper-card}
### Scaling Cooperative Mobile Multi-Robot Systems for Object Handling

Tobias Recker, Lukas Lachmayer, Annika Raatz. 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering (CASE), Los Angeles, CA, USA, pp. 2562-2567.

This paper presents and evaluates a scalable cooperative mobile multi-robot framework for object handling with multiple mobile manipulators.

<div class="link-list">
<a href="https://doi.org/10.1109/CASE58245.2025.11163753">DOI</a>
<a href="publications.html">More publications</a>
</div>
:::

## Dissertation

::: {.feature}
### Scaling of Cooperative Mobile Multi-Robot Systems for Handling and Assembly of Large-Scale Components

My dissertation investigates a scalable CMMRS framework for collaborative object handling and assembly tasks in industrial settings. It combines a hardware-agnostic control structure with path planning and experimental validation across transport, handling, and assembly-related scenarios.

<div class="link-list">
<a href="dissertation.html">Dissertation summary</a>
<a href="assets/pdfs/recker-dissertation-2025.pdf">Download PDF</a>
</div>
:::

## Research keywords

<ul class="tag-list">
<li>Mobile manipulation</li>
<li>Cooperative multi-robot systems</li>
<li>Formation control</li>
<li>Trajectory planning</li>
<li>Robot learning</li>
<li>Hardware and system integration</li>
<li>Construction automation</li>
</ul>
